title: Children's story spec
description: Machine-read spec for a children's story, written next to the story file as <basename>.spec.json. Declares the target age band, the educational takeaway, and the safety boundaries. The deterministic readability gate (scripts/check-readability.mjs) reads age_band to select word-count / sentence-length bounds (overridable via readability) and enforces safety.avoid. This is the children-story analogue of the SEO research sidecar / the novel state file: structured intent the gate can check.

story_id.description: Stable id, e.g. the story file basename.

age_band.description: Target reader age. Selects the readability bounds in check-readability.mjs.

educational_point.description: The single concrete takeaway the story teaches. Required — a children's story without a clear point is just noise.

safety.description: Child-safety boundaries the gate enforces deterministically.

avoid.description: Terms/topics that must NOT appear in the prose (case-insensitive substring match by the gate). Seed with age-inappropriate content for the band (e.g. graphic violence, weapons, scary death). An empty array is allowed but discouraged.

readability.description: OPTIONAL per-story override of the age-band defaults the gate carries. Omit to use the band defaults.
